name: Duluth Police Department Employees
description_short: CUScore is a directory and comparison tool for federally insured credit unions across America, providing NCUA financial data, health scores, and branch locations for 4,374 institutions.
description_long: CUScore is an independent online directory and comparison platform launched to help consumers research and compare credit unions across all 50 states, D.C., and U.S. territories. The platform aggregates quarterly financial data from the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) for over 4,300 federally insured credit unions, representing 146 million members and $2.5 trillion in combined assets. Rather than offering financial products directly, CUScore functions as an educational and research resource that enables consumers to evaluate credit unions by financial health scores, total assets, membership size, and state location. The platform positions itself as an independent entity, explicitly stating it is not affiliated with the NCUA or any individual credit union. CUScore distinguishes itself through its comprehensive comparative framework, allowing consumers to browse credit unions by state and access rankings of the largest institutions by assets. The platform emphasizes member education through free guides covering credit union fundamentals, credit union versus bank comparisons, and membership eligibility requirements. The site presents factual, NCUA-sourced data and maintains transparency about its data sourcing and update frequency (Q4 2025). However, as a directory rather than a credit union itself, CUScore cannot process member accounts, loans, or financial transactions—it serves purely an informational function. Users seeking actual credit union services must apply directly to member-owned institutions after researching options through this platform.
services: Search and browse 4,374 federally insured credit unions by state location
View top 10 largest credit unions ranked by total assets with health scores
Access NCUA quarterly financial data (Q4 2025) and industry statistics
Compare credit union versus bank features, rates, fees, and insurance coverage
Read free educational guides on credit union basics and membership eligibility
View nationwide membership totals and combined industry asset information
Sort and filter credit unions by state with aggregate asset totals
Access credit union health scores and financial ratios (Net Worth Ratio)
Review popular credit union options with member counts and assets
Read guides on how to join a credit union and find membership requirements
Explore credit union industry rankings and comparative analysis tools
View member-owned cooperative versus shareholder-owned bank structural differences
pros: Comprehensive directory of 4,374 federally insured credit unions with NCUA data and health scores
Free educational guides comparing credit unions versus banks and explaining membership requirements
Browse and filter credit unions by all 50 states, D.C., and U.S. territories
Top 10 rankings of largest credit unions by total assets with health ratings (A+, A, B+, B)
Access to Q4 2025 NCUA quarterly call report data updated March 2026
Shows member counts and total assets for transparency and financial comparison
Independent platform explicitly states it is not affiliated with NCUA or any individual credit union
cons: CUScore is a directory only—cannot open accounts, process loans, or conduct financial transactions
Limited feature set compared to actual credit union websites; no account management tools available
State-level aggregate data shown as $0 totals on main browsing interface, which is confusing and uninformative
Does not provide individual credit union detailed profiles, rates, fees, or product comparisons
Membership eligibility filters not available—users must research individual credit union requirements separately
best_for: Consumers researching which credit union to join in their state or nationwide
People comparing financial institutions and evaluating credit unions versus banks
Individuals seeking educational information about credit union structure, benefits, and membership
Researchers and analysts studying the credit union industry and institutional financial health
diagnosis: CUScore is best for consumers and researchers seeking to evaluate and compare credit unions across America using NCUA financial data and health scores before choosing where to apply for membership. The critical caveat is that CUScore is a research and directory platform only—it does not itself offer credit union services, accounts, loans, or products; users must apply directly to actual credit union institutions identified through this site.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
